name: Elementary Algebra (no credit)
description: Basic concepts of algebra, including polynomials, factoring, equations, graphs, and inequalities. Offered only if sufficient number of students enroll.

name: Trigonometry (no credit)
description: Basic concepts of trigonometry, including trigonometric functions, identities, inverse functions, and applications. Offered only if sufficient number of students enroll.

name: Intermediate Algebra (no credit)
description: Basic concepts of algebra, prepares student for college work in mathematics, such as MAT 016A or MAT 021A. Functions, equations, graphs, logarithms, and systems of equations. Offered only if sufficient number of students enroll.

name: Precalculus
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below. Topics selected for their use in calculus, including functions and their graphs, slope, zeroes of polynomials, exponential, logarithmic and trigonometric functions, sketching surfaces and solids.
rawPrerequisitesText: Two years of high school algebra, plane geometry, plane trigonometry; obtaining required score on the Precalculus Diagnostic Examination.

name: Calculus for Biology & Medicine
description: Introduction to differential calculus via applications in biology and medicine. Limits, derivatives of polynomials, trigonometric, and exponential functions, graphing, applications of the derivative to biology and medicine.
rawPrerequisitesText: Two years of high school algebra, plane geometry, plane trigonometry, and analytical geometry, and satisfying the Mathematics Placement Requirement.

name: Calculus for Biology & Medicine
description: Introduction to integral calculus and elementary differential equations via applications to biology and medicine. Fundamental theorem of calculus, techniques of integration including integral tables and numerical methods, improper integrals, elementary first order differential equations, applications in biology and medicine.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 016A C- or better or MAT 017A C- or better or MAT 019A C- or better or MAT 021A C- or better or MAT 021AH C- or better.

name: Calculus for Biology & Medicine
description: Matrix algebra, functions of several variables, partial derivatives, systems of differential equations, and applications to biology and medicine.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 017B C- or better.

name: Calculus for Data-Driven Applications
description: Calculus and other mathematical methods necessary in data driven analysis in the sciences, technology and the humanities.
rawPrerequisitesText: Two years of high school algebra, plane geometry, plane trigonometry, and satisfying the Mathematics Placement Requirement.

name: Calculus for Data-Driven Applications
description: Calculus and other mathematical methods necessary in data driven analysis in the sciences, technology, and the humanities.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 017A C- or better or MAT 021A C- or better or MAT 021AH C- or better or MAT 019A C- or better.

name: Calculus for Data-Driven Applications
description: Calculus and other mathematical methods necessary in data-driven analysis in the sciences, technology and the humanities. Multivariable calculus, differential equations, partial derivatives.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 019B C- or better or MAT 017B C- or better or MAT 021B C- or better or MAT 021BH C- or better.

name: Calculus
description: Functions, limits, continuity. Slope and derivative. Differentiation of algebraic and transcendental functions. Applications to motion, natural growth, graphing, extrema of a function. Differentials. L'Hopital's rule.
rawPrerequisitesText: Two years of high school algebra, plane geometry, plane trigonometry, and analytical geometry, and satisfying the Mathematics Placement Requirement.

name: Honors Calculus
description: More intensive treatment of material covered in MAT 021A.
rawPrerequisitesText: A Precalculus Diagnostic Examination score significantly higher than the minimum for MAT 021A is required.

name: Emerging Scholars Program Calculus Workshop
description: Functions, limits, continuity. Slope and derivative. Same course content as MAT 021A. Enrollment for students in the Emerging Scholars Program by instructor's invitation only.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 021A required concurrently.

name: Calculus
description: Continuation of MAT 021A. Definition of definite integral, fundamental theorem of calculus, techniques of integration. Application to area, volume, arc length, average of a function, improper integral, surface of revolution.
rawPrerequisitesText: (MAT 021A C- or better or MAT 021AH C- or better) or MAT 017A B or better or MAT 019A B or better.

name: Honors Calculus
description: More intensive treatment of material covered in MAT 021B. Students completing MAT 021BH can continue with MAT 021CH or the regular MAT 021C.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 021A B or better or MAT 021AH B or better.

name: Emerging Scholars Program Calculus Workshop
description: Continuation of MAT 021A. Same course content as MAT 021B. Enrollment for students in the Emerging Scholars Program by instructor's invitation only.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 021B required concurrently.

name: Calculus
description: Continuation of MAT 021B. Sequences, series, tests for convergence, Taylor expansions. Vector algebra, vector calculus, scalar and vector fields. Partial derivatives, total differentials. Applications to maximum and minimum problems in two or more variables. Applications to physical systems.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 016C C- or better or MAT 017C C- or better or MAT 017B B or better or MAT 019C C- or better or MAT 021B C- or better or MAT 021BH C- or better.

name: Honors Calculus
description: More intensive treatment of material covered in MAT 021C.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 021B B or better or MAT 021BH B or better.

name: Emerging Scholars Program Calculus Workshop
description: Continuation of MAT 021B. Same course content as MAT 021C. Enrollment for students in the Emerging Scholars Program by instructor's invitation only.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 021C required concurrently.

name: Vector Analysis
description: Continuation of MAT 021C. Definite integrals over plane and solid regions in various coordinate systems. Line and surface integrals. Green's theorem, Stoke's theorem, divergence theorem.
rawPrerequisitesText: (MAT 021C C- or better or MAT 021CH C- or better) or MAT 017C B or better.

name: Accelerated Calculus
description: Accelerated treatment of material from MAT 021A and MAT 021B, with detailed presentation of theory, definitions, and proofs, and treatment of computational aspects of calculus at a condensed but sophisticated level.
rawPrerequisitesText: Grade of B or higher in both semesters of high school calculus or a score of 4 or higher on the Advanced Placement Calculus AB exam, and obtaining the required score on the Precalculus Diagnostic Examination and its trigonometric component.

name: Linear Algebra
description: Matrices and linear transformations, determinants, eigenvalues, eigenvectors, diagonalization, factorization.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 017C C- or better or MAT 019C C- or better or MAT 021C C- or better or MAT 021CH C- or better.

name: Linear Algebra Computer Laboratory
description: Introduction to MATLAB and its use in linear algebra.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 016C or MAT 017C or MAT 021C or MAT 021CH.

name: Differential Equations
description: Solutions of elementary differential equations.
rawPrerequisitesText: (MAT 022A C- or better or MAT 027A C- or better or BIS 027A C- or better or MAT 067 C- or better).

name: Linear Algebra with Applications to Biology
description: Introduction to linear algebra with biological, medical, and bioengineering applications. Matrix algebra, vector spaces, orthogonality, determinants, eigenvalues, eigenvectors, principal component analysis, singular value decomposition, and linear transformations. Computer labs cover mathematical and computational techniques for modeling biological systems.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 017C C- or better or MAT 021C C- or better or MAT 021CH C- or better.

name: Differential Equations with Applications to Biology
description: Solutions of differential equations with biological, medical, and bioengineering applications. First and second order linear equations, phase plane analysis, nonlinear dynamics, Laplace transforms, and the diffusion equation. Computer labs cover mathematical and numerical techniques for modeling biological systems.
rawPrerequisitesText: (MAT 027A C- or better or BIS 027A C- or better) or (MAT 022A C- or better,  (MAT 022AL C- or better or ENG 006 C- or better or ECS 032A C- or better or ECS 032AV C- or better or ECS 036A C- or better or ECH 060 C- or better or EME 005 C- or better)).

name: Fundamentals of Mathematics
description: Introduction to fundamental mathematical ideas selected from the principal areas of modern mathematics. Properties of the primes, the fundamental theorems of arithmetic, properties of the rationals and irrationals, binary and other number systems.
rawPrerequisitesText: Satisfaction of the Mathematics Placement Requirement.

name: Modern Linear Algebra
description: Rigorous treatment of linear algebra; topics include vector spaces, bases and dimensions, orthogonal projections, eigenvalues and eigenvectors, similarity transformations, singular value decomposition and positive definiteness.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 021C C- or better or MAT 021CH C- or better.

name: Explorations in Elementary Mathematics
description: Weekly explorations of mathematical ideas related to the elementary school curriculum will be carried out by cooperative learning groups. Lectures will provide background and synthesize the results of group exploration.
rawPrerequisitesText: Two years of high school mathematics.

name: Explorations in Elementary Mathematics
description: Weekly explorations of mathematical ideas related to the elementary school curriculum will be carried out by cooperative learning groups. Lectures will provide background and synthesize the results of group exploration.
rawPrerequisitesText: Two years of high school mathematics.

name: Elementary Problem Solving
description: Solve and present solutions to challenging and interesting problems in elementary mathematics.
rawPrerequisitesText: High school mathematics through precalculus.

name: Directed Group Study
description: Directed group study.
rawPrerequisitesText: Consent of instructor.

name: Special Study for Undergraduates
description: Special study for undergraduates.
rawPrerequisitesText: Consent of instructor.

name: Probability & Stochastic Processes with Applications to Biology
description: Introduction to probability theory and stochastic processes with biological, medical, and bioengineering applications. Combinatorics, discrete and continuous random variables, Bayes’ formula, conditional probability, Markov chains, Poisson processes, and Brownian motion. Computer labs cover mathematical and computational modeling techniques.
rawPrerequisitesText: (MAT 027A C- or better or BIS 027A C- or better) or (MAT 022A C- or better,  (MAT 022AL C- or better or ENG 006 C- or better or ECS 032A C- or better or ECS 032AV C- or better or ECS 036A C- or better or ECH 060 C- or better or EME 005 C- or better)).

name: Introduction to Abstract Mathematics
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below. Rigorous treatment of mathematical concepts with emphasis on developing the ability to understand abstract mathematical ideas, to read and write mathematical concepts, and to prove theorems. Designed to serve as preparation for the more rigorous upper division courses.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 021B.

name: Introduction to Abstract Mathematics
description: Rigorous treatment of mathematical concepts with emphasis on developing the ability to understand abstract mathematical ideas, to read and write mathematical concepts, and to prove theorems. Designed to serve as preparation for the more rigorous upper division courses.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 021B.

name: History of Mathematics
description: History of mathematics from ancient times through the development of calculus. Mathematics from Arab, Hindu, Chinese and other cultures. Selected topics from the history of modern mathematics.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 127A or MAT 067 or MAT 108 or MAT 108V or MAT 114 or MAT 115A or MAT 141 or MAT 145; 8 units of upper division Mathematics.

name: Convex Geometry
description: Topics selected from the theory of convex bodies, convex functions, geometric inequalities, combinatorial geometry, and integral geometry. Designed to serve as preparation for the more rigorous upper division courses.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 021C; (MAT 022A or BIS 027A or MAT 067).

name: Number Theory
description: Divisibility and related topics, diophantine equations, selected topics from the theory of prime numbers. Designed to serve as preparation for the more rigorous upper division courses.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 021B.

name: Number Theory
description: Euler function, Moebius function, congruences, primitive roots, quadratic reciprocity law.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 115A; (MAT 022A or MAT 027A or MAT 067 or BIS 027A).

name: Differential Geometry
description: Vector analysis, curves, and surfaces in three dimensions.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 021D; (MAT 022A or MAT 027A or MAT 067 or BIS 027A); (MAT 022B or MAT 027B or BIS 027B).

name: Partial Differential Equations: Elementary Methods
description: Derivation of partial differential equations; separation of variables; equilibrium solutions and Laplace's equation; Fourier series; method of characteristics for the one dimensional wave equation. Solution of nonhomogeneous equations.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 021D; (MAT 022A or MAT 027A or MAT 067 or BIS 027A); (MAT 022B or MAT 027B or BIS 027B).

name: Partial Differential Equations: Eigenfunction Expansions
description: Sturm-Liouville Theory; selfadjoint operators; mixed boundary conditions; partial differential equations in two and three dimensions; Eigenvalue problems in circular domains; nonhomogeneous problems and the method of eigenfunction expansions; Poisson's Equations.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 118A.

name: Partial Differential Equations: Green's Functions & Transforms
description: Green's functions for one-dimensional problems and Poisson's equation; Fourier transforms; Green's Functions for time dependent problems; Laplace transform and solution of partial differential equations.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 118B.

name: Ordinary Differential Equations
description: Scalar and planar autonomous systems; nonlinear systems and linearization; existence and uniqueness of solutions; matrix solution of linear systems; phase plane analysis; stability analysis; bifurcation theory; Liapunov's method; limit cycles; Poincare Bendixon theory.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 021D; (MAT 022A or MAT 027A or MAT 067 or BIS 027A); (MAT 022B or MAT 027B or BIS 027B).

name: Ordinary Differential Equations
description: Lorentz equations; Poincare maps; center manifolds and normal forms; scalar and planar maps; phase space analysis for iterated maps; period-doubling bifurcation; Lyapunov exponent; chaos and symbolic dynamics; strange attractors; fractals.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 119A.

name: Mathematical Biology
description: Methods of mathematical modeling of biological systems including difference equations, ordinary differential equations, stochastic and dynamic programming models. Computer simulation methods applied to biological systems. Applications to population growth, cell biology, physiology, evolutionary ecology and protein clustering. MATLAB programming required.
rawPrerequisitesText: (MAT 022A or MAT 027A or MAT 067 or BIS 027A); (MAT 022B or MAT 027B or BIS 027B).

name: Real Analysis
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below. Real numbers, sequences, series, and continuous functions.
rawPrerequisitesText: (MAT 021C or MAT 021CH); (MAT 067 or MAT 022A or MAT 027A or BIS 027A); (MAT 108 or MAT 108V).

name: Real Analysis
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below. Derivatives, integrals, sequences of functions, and power series.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 127A.

name: Real Analysis
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below. Metric spaces and multi-variable calculus.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 127B.

name: Numerical Analysis
description: Error analysis, approximation, interpolation, numerical differentiation and integration.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 021C; (ECS 032A or ECS 032AV or ENG 006); programming in languages such as MATLAB or Python will be used.

name: Numerical Analysis in Solution of Equations
description: Solution of nonlinear equations and nonlinear systems. Minimization of functions of several variables. Simultaneous linear equations. Eigenvalue problems. Linear programming.
rawPrerequisitesText: (ECS 032A or ECS 032AV or ENG 006); (MAT 022A or MAT 027A or MAT 067 or BIS 027A); methods of programming in languages such as MATLAB or Python for numerical analysis will be used.

name: Numerical Analysis in Differential Equations
description: Difference equations, operators, numerical solutions of ordinary and partial differential equations.
rawPrerequisitesText: (ECS 032A or ECS 032AV or ENG 006); (MAT 022A or MAT 027A or MAT 067 or BIS 027A); (MAT 022B or MAT 027B or BIS 027B); methods of programming in languages such as MATLAB or Python for numerical analysis will be used.

name: Fourier Analysis
description: Fourier series and integrals, orthogonal sets of functions. Topics selected from trigonometric approximation, orthogonal polynomials, applications to signal and image processing, numerical analysis, and differential equations.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 021D; MAT 127A; (MAT 022A or MAT 027A or MAT 067 or BIS 027A); (MAT 022B or MAT 027B or BIS 027B):

name: Mathematical Finance
description: Analysis and evaluation of deterministic and random cash flow streams, yield and pricing of basic financial instruments, interest rate theory, mean-variance portfolio theory, capital asset pricing models, utility functions and general principles. MATLAB programming required.
rawPrerequisitesText: (MAT 067 or MAT 022A or MAT 027A or BIS 027A); MAT 108; MAT 135A

name: Probability
description: Probability space; discrete probability, combinatorial analysis; independence, conditional probability; random variables, discrete and continuous distributions, probability mass function, joint and marginal density functions; expectation, moments, variance, Chebyshev inequality; sums of random variables, random walk, large number law, central limit theorem.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 021C; (MAT 108 or MAT 108V or MAT 067).

name: Stochastic Processes
description: Generating functions, branching processes, characteristic function; Markov chains; convergence of random variables, law of iterated logarithm; random processes, Brownian motion, stationary processes, renewal processes, queueing theory, martingales.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 135A; (MAT 022A  or MAT 027A or MAT 067 or BIS 027A).

name: Euclidean Geometry
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below. Axiomatic and analytic examination of Euclidean geometry from an advanced point of view. In particular, a discussion of its relation to other geometries. Designed to serve as preparation for the more rigorous upper division courses.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 021B; (MAT 022A or MAT 027A or MAT 067 or BIS 027A).

name: Combinatorics
description: Combinatorial methods using basic graph theory, counting methods, generating functions, and recurrence relations. Designed to serve as preparation for the more rigorous upper division courses.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 021C.

name: Algebraic Combinatorics
description: Enumeration, Polya theory, generating functions, current topics in algebraic combinatorics.
rawPrerequisitesText: ((MAT 022A or MAT 027A or BIS 027A,  MAT 108) or MAT 067)); MAT 145.

name: Topology
description: Basic notions of point-set and combinatorial topology.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 127A.

name: Discrete Mathematics
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below. Coding theory, error correcting codes, finite fields and the algebraic concepts needed in their development.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 067 or (MAT 022A or MAT 027A or BIS 027A, (MAT 108 or MAT 108V)).

name: Modern Algebra
description: Basic concepts of groups, symmetries of the plane. Emphasis on the techniques used in the proof of the ideas (Lemmas, Theorems, etc.) developing these concepts. Precise thinking, proof writing, and the ability to deal with abstraction.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 067 or (MAT 022A or MAT 027A or BIS 027A, (MAT 108 or MAT 108V)).

name: Modern Algebra
description: Bilinear forms, rings, factorization, modules.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 150A.

name: Modern Algebra
description: Group representations, fields, Galois theory.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 150B.

name: Mathematics & Computers
description: Introduction to computational mathematics, symbolic computation, and computer generated/verified proofs in algebra, analysis and geometry. Investigation of rigorous new mathematics developed in conjunction with modern computational questions and the role that computers play in mathematical conjecture and experimentation.
rawPrerequisitesText: (MAT 127A or MAT 108 or MAT 108V or MAT 114 or MAT 115A or MAT 145); (MAT 022A or MAT 027A or MAT 067 or BIS 027A).

name: Applied Linear Algebra
description: Applications of linear algebra; LU and QR matrix factorizations, eigenvalue and singular value matrix decompositions.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 022A or MAT 027A or BIS 027A or MAT 067.

name: Optimization
description: Linear programming, simplex method. Basic properties of unconstrained nonlinear problems, descent methods, conjugate direction method. Constrained minimization. Programming language required.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 021C; (((MAT 022A or MAT 027A or BIS 027A), (MAT 108 or MAT 108V)) or MAT 067).

name: Mathematics for Data Analytics & Decision Making
description: Relational model; relational algebra, relational calculus, normal forms, functional and multivalued dependencies, separability. Cost benefit analysis of physical database design and reorganization. Performance via analytical modeling, simulation, and queueing theory. Block accesses; buffering; operating system contention; CPU intensive operations.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 167 or MAT 128B or ECS 130.

name: Special Topics
description: Special topics from various fields of modern, pure, and applied mathematics. Some recent topics include Knot Theory, General Relativity, and Fuzzy Sets.
rawPrerequisitesText: (MAT 067 or (MAT 022A or MAT 027A or BIS 027A, MAT 108)), MAT 127A.

name: Complex Analysis
description: Complex number system, analyticity and the Cauchy-Riemann equations, elementary functions, complex integration, power and Laurent series expansions, residue theory.
rawPrerequisitesText: (MAT 067 or (MAT 022A or MAT 027A or BIS 027A, MAT 108)), MAT 127B.

name: Complex Analysis
description: Analytical functions, elementary functions and their mapping properties, applications of Cauchy's integral theorem, conformal mapping and applications to heat flow and fluid mechanics.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 185A.

name: Advanced Problem Solving
description: Solution and presentation of advanced problem solving techniques. Solve and present interesting and challenging problems of all areas of mathematics.
rawPrerequisitesText: (((MAT 022A, MAT 027A or BIS 027A,) (MAT 108 or MAT 108V)) or MAT 067)); MAT 127A.

name: Internship in Applied Mathematics
description: Supervised work experience in applied mathematics. Final report.
rawPrerequisitesText: Consent of instructor.

name: Undergraduate Thesis
description: Independent research under supervision of a faculty member. Student will submit written report in thesis form.
rawPrerequisitesText: Consent of instructor.

name: Tutoring Mathematics in the Community
description: Special projects in mathematical education developing techniques for mathematics instruction and tutoring on an individual or small group basis.
rawPrerequisitesText: Consent of instructor.

name: Directed Group Study
description: Directed group study.
rawPrerequisitesText: Consent of instructor.

name: Special Study for Advanced Undergraduates
description: Special study for advanced undergraduates.

name: Problem-Solving in Analysis
description: Problem-solving in graduate analysis: continuous functions, metric spaces, Banach & Hilbert spaces, bounded linear operators, the spectral theorem, distributions, Fourier series & transforms, Lp spaces, Sobolev spaces.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 201A (can be concurrent); MAT 201B (can be concurrent).

name: Analysis
description: Metric and normed spaces. Continuous functions. Topological, Hilbert, and Banach spaces. Fourier series. Spectrum of bounded and compact linear operators. Linear differential operators and Green's functions. Distributions. Fourier transform. Measure theory. Lp and Sobolev spaces. Differential calculus and variational methods.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing in Mathematics or Applied Mathematics, or consent of instructor.

name: Analysis
description: Metric and normed spaces. Continuous functions. Topological, Hilbert, and Banach spaces. Fourier series. Spectrum of bounded and compact linear operators. Linear differential operators and Green's functions. Distributions. Fourier transform. Measure theory. Lp and Sobolev spaces. Differential calculus and variational methods.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing in Mathematics or Applied Mathematics, or consent of instructor.

name: Analysis
description: Metric and normed spaces. Continuous functions. Topological, Hilbert, and Banach spaces. Fourier series. Spectrum of bounded and compact linear operators. Linear differential operators and Green's functions. Distributions. Fourier transform. Measure theory. Lp and Sobolev spaces. Differential calculus and variational methods.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing in Mathematics or Applied Mathematics, or consent of instructor.

name: Functional Analysis
description: Hahn-Banach, Open mapping, Closed graph, Banach-Steinhaus, and Krein-Milman. Subspaces and quotient spaces. Projections. Weak and weak-star topologies. Compact and adjoint operators in Banach spaces. Fredholm theory. Functions of operators. Spectral theory of self-adjoint operators.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 201A; MAT 201B.

name: Complex Analysis
description: Analytic continuation, Riemann surfaces, conformal mappings, Riemann mapping theorem, entire functions, special functions, elliptic functions.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 185A; or equivalent to MAT 185A, or consent of instructor.

name: Complex Analysis
description: Cauchy's theorem, Cauchy's integral formulas, meromorphic functions, complex logarithm, entire functions, Weierstrass infinite product formula, the gamma and zeta functions, and prime number theorem.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 185A; or equivalent to MAT 185A, or consent of instructor.

name: Complex Analysis
description: Conformal mappings, the Schwarz lemma, analytic automorphisms, the Riemann mapping theorem, elliptic functions, Eisenstein series, the Jacobi theta functions, asymptotics, Bessel functions, the Airy function, topics on special functions and Riemann surfaces.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 205A; or consent of instructor.

name: Measure Theory
description: Introduction to measure theory. The study of lengths, surface areas, and volumes in general spaces, as related to integration theory.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 125B.

name: Methods of Applied Mathematics
description: Ordinary differential equations and dynamical systems. Variational principles. Eigenfunctions, integral equations and Green's functions. Complex analysis and contour integration. Laplace's equation. Diffusion equations. Wave phenomena. Dimensional analysis and scaling. Asymptotic expansions and perturbation theory. Stochastic processes and Brownian motion.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing or consent of instructor.

name: Methods of Applied Mathematics
description: Ordinary differential equations and dynamical systems. Variational principles. Eigenfunctions, integral equations and Green's functions. Complex analysis and contour integration. Laplace's equation. Diffusion equations. Wave phenomena. Dimensional analysis and scaling. Asymptotic expansions and perturbation theory. Stochastic processes and Brownian motion.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing or consent of instructor.

name: Methods of Applied Mathematics
description: Ordinary differential equations and dynamical systems. Variational principles. Eigenfunctions, integral equations and Green's functions. Complex analysis and contour integration. Laplace's equation. Diffusion equations. Wave phenomena. Dimensional analysis and scaling. Asymptotic expansions and perturbation theory. Stochastic processes and Brownian motion.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing or consent of instructor.

name: Topology
description: Fundamental group and covering space theory. Homology and cohomology. Manifolds and duality. CW complexes. Fixed point theorems.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing or consent of instructor.

name: Topology
description: Fundamental group and covering space theory. Homology and cohomology. Manifolds and duality. CW complexes. Fixed point theorems.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing or consent of instructor.

name: Topology
description: Fundamental group and covering space theory. Homology and cohomology. Manifolds and duality. CW complexes. Fixed point theorems.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing or consent of instructor.

name: Geometric Topology
description: Topology of two- and three-dimensional manifolds. Surfaces and their diffeomorphisms. Dehn twists. Heegaard surfaces. Theory of 3-dimensional manifolds. Knots and knot theory. Hyperbolic manifolds and geometric structures.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 215A.

name: Partial Differential Equations
description: Year-long sequence on PDEs which covers linear transport, Laplace, heat, and wave equations, maximum principles, method of characteristics, Sobelev and Hölder space theory, weak derivatives, semilinear, quasilinear, and fully nonlinear elliptic/parabolic equations, nonlinear hyperbolic equations, and compensated compactness.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 201A; MAT 201B; MAT 201C; or consent of instructor.

name: Partial Differential Equations
description: Year-long sequence on PDEs which covers linear transport, Laplace, heat, and wave equations, maximum principles, method of characteristics, Sobelev and Hölder space theory, weak derivatives, semilinear, quasilinear, and fully nonlinear elliptic/parabolic equations, nonlinear hyperbolic equations, and compensated compactness.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 218A; or consent of instructor.

name: Partial Differential Equations
description: Year-long sequence on PDEs which covers linear transport, Laplace, heat, and wave equations, maximum principles, method of characteristics, Sobelev and Hölder space theory, weak derivatives, semilinear, quasilinear, and fully nonlinear elliptic/parabolic equations, nonlinear hyperbolic equations, and compensated compactness.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 218B; or consent of instructor.

name: Mathematical Fluid Dynamics
description: Kinematics and dynamics of fluids. The Euler and Navier-Stokes equations. Vorticity dynamics. Irrotational flow. Low Reynolds number flows and the Stokes equations. High Reynolds number flows and boundary layers. Compressible fluids. Shock waves.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 118B; or consent of instructor.

name: Mathematical Fluid Dynamics
description: Kinematics and dynamics of fluids. The Euler and Navier-Stokes equations. Vorticity dynamics. Irrotational flow. Low Reynolds number flows and the Stokes equations. High Reynolds number flows and boundary layers. Compressible fluids. Shock waves.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 118B; or consent of instructor.

name: Numerical Methods: Fundamentals
description: Fundamental principles and methods in numerical analysis, including the concepts of stability of algorithms and conditioning of numerical problems, numerical methods for interpolation and integration, eigenvalue problems, singular value decomposition and its applications.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 128A; MAT 128B; or equivalent, or consent of instructor; familiarity with some programming language.

name: Numerical Methods: Large-Scale Matrix Computations
description: Numerical methods for large-scale matrix computations, including direct and iterative methods for the solution of linear systems, the computation of eigenvalues and singular values, the solution of least-squares problems, matrix compression, methods for the solution of linear programs.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 167; or equivalent, or consent of instructor; familiarity with some programming language.

name: Numerical Methods: Ordinary Differential Equations
description: Numerical methods for the solution of ordinary differential equations, including methods for initial-value problems and two-point boundary-value problems, theory of and methods for differential algebraic equations, dimension reduction of large-scale dynamical systems.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 022B; or equivalent, or consent of instructor; familiarity with some programming language.

name: Mathematical Biology
description: Nonlinear ordinary and partial differential equations and stochastic processes of cell and molecular biology. Scaling, qualitative, and numerical analysis of mathematical models. Applications to nerve impulse, chemotaxis, muscle contraction, and morphogenesis.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing or consent of instructor.

name: Numerical Solution of Differential Equations
description: Numerical solutions of initial-value, eigenvalue and boundary-value problems for ordinary differential equations. Numerical solution of parabolic and hyperbolic partial differential equations.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 128C.

name: Numerical Solution of Differential Equations
description: Numerical solutions of initial-value, eigenvalue and boundary-value problems for ordinary differential equations. Numerical solution of parabolic and hyperbolic partial differential equations.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 128C.

name: Numerical Solution of Differential Equations
description: Numerical solutions of initial-value, eigenvalue and boundary-value problems for ordinary differential equations. Numerical solution of parabolic and hyperbolic partial differential equations.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 128C.

name: Probability Theory
description: Measure-theoretic foundations, abstract integration, independence, laws of large numbers, characteristic functions, central limit theorems. Weak convergence in metric spaces, Brownian motion, invariance principle. Conditional expectation. Topics selected from: martingales, Markov chains, ergodic theory.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 125B; (MAT 135A or STA 131A); or consent of instructor.

name: Probability Theory
description: Measure-theoretic foundations, abstract integration, independence, laws of large numbers, characteristic functions, central limit theorems. Weak convergence in metric spaces, Brownian motion, invariance principle. Conditional expectation. Topics selected from: martingales, Markov chains, ergodic theory.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 235A or STA 235A; or consent of instructor.

name: Probability Theory
description: Measure-theoretic foundations, abstract integration, independence, laws of large numbers, characteristic functions, central limit theorems. Weak convergence in metric spaces, Brownian motion, invariance principle. Conditional expectation. Topics selected from: martingales, Markov chains, ergodic theory.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 235B or STA 235B; or consent of instructor.

name: Stochastic Dynamics & Applications
description: Stochastic processes, Brownian motion, Stochastic integration, martingales, stochastic differential equations. Diffusions, connections with partial differential equations, mathematical finance.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 201C or (MAT 235B or STA 235B); MAT 235A, MAT 235B, MAT 235C/STA 235A, STA 235B, STA 235C recommended.

name: Stochastic Dynamics & Applications
description: Stochastic processes, Brownian motion, Stochastic integration, martingales, stochastic differential equations. Diffusions, connections with partial differential equations, mathematical finance.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 201C or (MAT 235B or STA 235B); MAT 235A, MAT 235B, MAT 235C/STA 235A, STA 235B, STA 235C recommended.

name: Differential Topology
description: Differentiable manifolds, vector fields, transverse intersections, Sard's Theorem, orientations, intersection theory, the index of a vector field, differential forms, integration on manifolds, Stokes' Theorem, deRham cohomology, Morse functions, the Morse lemma, and the index of critical points.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 201A; or consent of instructor; Vector calculus, point-set topology; MAT 250A  MAT 250B highly recommended.

name: Differential Geometry
description: Riemannian metrics, connections, geodesics, Gauss lemma, convex neighborhoods, curvature tensor, Ricci and scalar curvature, connections and curvature on vector bundles.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 201A; MAT 239; MAT 250A  MAT 250B highly recommended; intended primarily for second-year graduate students.

name: Differential Geometry
description: Jacobi fields, conjugate points, completeness, Hopf-Rinow theorem, Cartan-Hadamard theorem, energy, variation theorems and their applications, Rauch comparison theorem and its applications.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 240A; Intended primarily for second-year graduate students.

name: Enumerative Combinatorics
description: Introduction to modern combinatorics and its applications. Emphasis on enumerative aspects of combinatorial theory.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 145; MAT 150; or the equivalent, or consent of instructor.

name: Algebraic Combinatorics
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below. Algebraic and geometric aspects of combinatorics. Use of structures such as groups, polytopes, rings, and simplicial complexes to solve combinatorial problems.

name: Algebraic Geometry
description: Affine varieties and radical ideals. Projective varieties. Abstract varieties. Morphisms and rational maps. Smoothness. Algebraic curves and the Riemann-Roch theorem. Special topics.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 250A; MAT 250B; MAT 250C.

name: Algebraic Geometry
description: Complex varieties and the analytic topology. Sheaves and schemes. Fiber products. Separatedness and properness. Applications of scheme theory.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 248A.

name: Problem-Solving in Algebra
description: Problem-solving in graduate algebra: groups, rings, modules, matrices, tensor products, representations, Galois theory, ring extensions, commutative algebra and homological algebra.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 250A (can be concurrent); MAT 250B (can be concurrent).

name: Algebra
description: Group and rings. Sylow theorems, abelian groups, Jordan-Holder theorem. Rings, unique factorization. Algebras, and modules. Fields and vector spaces over fields. Field extensions. Commutative rings. Representation theory and its applications.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing in mathematics or consent of instructor.

name: Algebra
description: Group and rings. Sylow theorems, abelian groups, Jordan-Holder theorem. Rings, unique factorization. Algebras, and modules. Fields and vector spaces over fields. Field extensions. Commutative rings. Representation theory and its applications.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing in mathematics or consent of instructor.

name: Algebra
description: Group and rings. Sylow theorems, abelian groups, Jordan-Holder theorem. Rings, unique factorization. Algebras, and modules. Fields and vector spaces over fields. Field extensions. Commutative rings. Representation theory and its applications.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing in mathematics or consent of instructor.

name: Numerical Optimization
description: Numerical methods for infinite dimensional optimization problems. Newton and Quasi-Newton methods, linear and sequential quadratic programming, barrier methods; large-scale optimization; theory of approximations; infinite and semi-infinite programming; applications to optimal control, stochastic optimization and distributed systems.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 025; MAT 167.

name: Discrete & Mixed-Integer Optimization
description: Combinatorial, integer, and mixed-integer linear optimization problems. Ideal and strong formulations, cutting planes, branch and cut, decomposition methods.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 025; MAT 167; or consent of instructor.

name: Lie Groups & Lie Algebras
description: Lie groups, examples and topological properties. Lie algebras and representation theory, and semisimple Lie algebras.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 147 and MAT 150A strongly encouraged; or equivalent; MAT 250A and MAT 215A and MAT 239 (can be concurrent) recommended.

name: Mathematical Quantum Mechanics
description: Mathematical foundations of quantum mechanics: the Hilbert space and Operator Algebra formulations; the Schrödinger and Heisenberg equations, symmetry in quantum mechanics, basics of spectral theory and perturbation theory. Applications to atoms and molecules. The Dirac equation.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 201; or consent of instructor.

name: Mathematical Statistical Mechanics & Quantum Field Theory
description: Mathematical principles of statistical mechanics and quantum field theory. Topics include classical and quantum lattice systems, variational principles, spontaneous symmetry breaking and phase transitions, second quantization and Fock space, and fundamentals of quantum field theory.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 265; or consent of instructor.

name: Quantum Information Theory
description: Axioms of quantum information, quantum states and observables, Bell non-locality, quantum channels, quantum entropy, quantum error correction, quantum circuits, examples of quantum algorithms.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 201B or MAT 250B; or consent of instructor.

name: Mathematics of Data Science
description: Mathematical methods and algorithms that are fundamental to a variety of data science applications. Theoretical foundations and inner workings of popular algorithms used in data science, machine learning, and artificial intelligence. Implementation and application of these algorithms to real-world data sets.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 127A; MAT 167; MAT 135A.

name: Applied & Computational Harmonic Analysis
description: Introduction to mathematical basic building blocks (wavelets, local Fourier basis, and their relatives) useful for diverse fields (signal and image processing, numerical analysis, and statistics). Emphasis on the connection between the continuum and the discrete worlds.
rawPrerequisitesText: (MAT 125B or MAT 201C); (MAT 128B or MAT 167); MAT 129; or the equivalent, or consent of instructor.

name: Topics in Pure & Applied Mathematics
description: Special topics in various fields of pure and applied mathematics. Topics selected based on the mutual interests of students and faculty.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing.

name: Seminar
description: Advanced study in various fields of mathematics, including analysis, applied mathematics, discrete mathematics, geometry, mathematical biology, mathematical physics, optimization, partial differential equations, probability, and topology.

name: Group Study
description: Group study.

name: Individual Study
description: Individual study.

name: Dissertation Research
description: Dissertation research.

name: Mathematics Teaching Practicum
description: Specialist training in mathematics teaching. Teaching, training, and cross observing classes taught using large group Socratic techniques, small group guided inquiry experiences, and/or other approaches to teaching at various grade levels. Required for advanced degrees in mathematics education.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 302A (can be concurrent); MAT 303A (can be concurrent); MAT 302A  MAT 303A required concurrently or consent of instructor.

name: Mathematics Teaching Practicum
description: Specialist training in mathematics teaching. Teaching, training, and cross observing classes taught using large group Socratic techniques, small group guided inquiry experiences, and/or other approaches to teaching at various grade levels. Required for advanced degrees in mathematics education.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 302B (can be concurrent); MAT 303B (can be concurrent); MAT 302B  MAT 303B required concurrently or consent of instructor.

name: Mathematics Teaching Practicum
description: Specialist training in mathematics teaching. Teaching, training, and cross observing classes taught using large group Socratic techniques, small group guided inquiry experiences, and/or other approaches to teaching at various grade levels. Required for advanced degrees in mathematics education.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 302C (can be concurrent); MAT 303B (can be concurrent); MAT 302C  MAT 303C required concurrently or consent of instructor.

name: Curriculum Development in Mathematics
description: Mathematics curriculum development for all grade levels. Required for advanced degrees in mathematics education.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 303A required concurrently or consent of instructor.

name: Curriculum Development in Mathematics
description: Mathematics curriculum development for all grade levels. Required for advanced degrees in mathematics education.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 303B required concurrently or consent of instructor.

name: Curriculum Development in Mathematics
description: Mathematics curriculum development for all grade levels. Required for advanced degrees in mathematics education.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 303C required concurrently or consent of instructor.

name: Mathematics Pedagogy
description: An investigation of the interplay of mathematical pedagogy and mathematical content, including a historical survey of past and present methods in view of some of the influences that shaped their development.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 302A or MAT 210AL required concurrently or consent of instructor.

name: Mathematics Pedagogy
description: An investigation of the interplay of mathematical pedagogy and mathematical content, including a historical survey of past and present methods in view of some of the influences that shaped their development.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 302A or MAT 210AL required concurrently or consent of instructor.

name: Mathematics Pedagogy
description: An investigation of the interplay of mathematical pedagogy and mathematical content, including a historical survey of past and present methods in view of some of the influences that shaped their development.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 302C or MAT 210CL required concurrently or consent of instructor.

name: Teaching Assistantship Training
description: Experience in methods of assisting and teaching of mathematics at the university level. Includes discussion of lecturing techniques, running discussion sessions, holding office hours, preparing and grading of examinations, student-teacher interaction, and related topics. Required of departmental teaching assistants.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing in the Department of Mathematics.

name: Individual Study
description: Individual study of some aspect of mathematics education or a focused work on a curriculum design project under supervision of a faculty member in mathematics.
